background: A study of environmental air quality measured suspended particulate matter in air samples at two sites. Data (μgms/m^3) for site 1 are {68, 22, 36, 32, 42, 24, 28, and 38}. Data for site 2 are {36, 38, 39, 40, 36, 34, 33, and 32}.

task.query: Create back-to-back stemplots of data from these sites. Discuss the results.
task.answer:
(a) Stemplots
```
Site 1| | Site 2
842 |2 |
862 |3 |2346689
2|4|0
    |5|
    8|6|
    \times10
```

Discussion:
- Site 1 has a high outlier.
- The distributions have similar central locations.
- Site 1 has greater variability.

task.query: Calculate group means and standard deviations.
task.answer:
(b) Means and standard deviations

\begin{tabular}{cccc}
\hline & $\boldsymbol{n}$ & Mean & Std. Dev. \\
1 & 8 & 36.25 & 14.56 \\
2 & 8 & 36.00 & 2.88 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

task.query: Summarize your graphical and numerical findings.
task.answer: (c) The summary statistics confirm that the distributions have similar central locations and that Site 1 has much greater variability.

data:
Table 12.9 "Air samples data" for Exercises 12.5 and 12.7. Data are suspended particulate matter in air samples ( $\mu \mathrm{gms} / \mathrm{m}^3$ ).

\begin{tabular}{lllllllll}
\hline Site 1: & 68 & 22 & 36 & 32 & 42 & 24 & 28 & 38 \\
Site 2: & 36 & 38 & 39 & 40 & 36 & 34 & 33 & 32 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Data are fictitious and are stored online in the file AIRSAMPLES.SAV.
